page.title: Azimut 39 vs Pershing 46 in Marbella: Size and Performance

data.meta_description: Compare the Azimut 39 vs Pershing 46 in Marbella: size, speed, and price. Which boat suits your charter? Book your day on the water today.
data.summary: A side-by-side comparison of the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 for boat charters in Marbella, covering size, performance, capacity, and pricing to help you choose the right vessel.
key_facts: Pershing 46 pricing: on request via WhatsApp.
Both boats include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Pershing 46 is longer and sportier; Azimut 39 offers more passenger capacity.
Azimut 39 is a budget-friendly option; Pershing 46 for performance seekers.

faq.q: Which boat is faster, the Azimut 39 or Pershing 46?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 is generally faster due to its sportier design and larger engines, reaching top speeds around 38-40 knots. The Azimut 39 cruises at about 28-30 knots, making the Pershing the better choice for thrill-seekers.

faq.q: How many people can the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 accommodate?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 holds up to 11 passengers, while the Pershing 46 accommodates up to 10. Both boats are certified for their maximum capacities, so you can bring a group of friends or family without worry.

faq.q: What is included in the price for each boat?
faq.a: Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Azimut 39 has fixed pricing from €749/2h to €2,299/8h; the Pershing 46 requires a request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boat is more spacious for a day charter?
faq.a: The Pershing 46 offers more deck space and a larger cockpit due to its 14m length, making it feel roomier. The Azimut 39 is slightly smaller but still comfortable, with a focus on social seating and a sunpad.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on these charters?
faq.a: Yes, you can bring additional food and drinks. However, the charter already includes a selection of beverages and light snacks. Check with us via WhatsApp if you have special requests.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a party or celebration?
faq.a: The Azimut 39, with capacity for 11 guests and a sociable layout, is ideal for parties. The Pershing 46, though slightly smaller in capacity, offers a sportier vibe and more deck space for mingling.

faq.q: Do both boats depart from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Yes, both the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. This gives you easy access to the Costa del Sol's best spots like Cabopino, Estepona, and the Marbella coastline.

faq.q: How do I book the Pershing 46 if its price is on request?
faq.a: Simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date and duration. We'll respond with availability and a tailored quote. The Azimut 39 can be booked directly online with fixed rates.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 for your Marbella boat charter can be tricky. Both are sleek, well-maintained vessels departing from Puerto Banús, but they cater to different priorities. This comparison breaks down their size, performance, capacity, and pricing to help you decide which fits your day on the Costa del Sol. Whether you're celebrating with friends or seeking a swift cruise along the coast, here's what you need to know.

Azimut 39 vs Pershing 46: Key Specs at a Glance

The Azimut 39 measures 12.5 metres and can carry up to 11 passengers. The Pershing 46 is longer at 14 metres but has a slightly lower capacity of 10 guests. Both boats depart from Puerto Banús, ensuring easy access to Marbella's coastline. The Azimut 39 is a flybridge model, offering a sun deck and social seating, while the Pershing 46 is an open sport cruiser with a more aggressive, performance-oriented design. For a detailed look at all our options, visit our [boat rental Marbella](/blog/azimut-39-vs-pershing-46-marbella/) page.

Performance and Speed

Speed is where the Pershing 46 shines. With twin engines pushing it to around 38-40 knots, it's built for those who want a thrilling ride. The Azimut 39 cruises comfortably at 28-30 knots, offering a smoother, more relaxed experience. If you're planning to cover more distance—say, from Puerto Banús to Estepona or Sotogrande—the Pershing's speed cuts travel time. However, for a leisurely day of swimming and sunbathing, the Azimut's steady pace is ample.

Space and Comfort

The Pershing 46's extra 1.5 metres in length translates to a larger cockpit and more deck space. Its open layout is perfect for sunbathing or socialising, with a generous sunpad aft. The Azimut 39, though compact, maximises space with a flybridge that offers panoramic views of La Concha and the Marbella coastline. Both boats have a cabin below deck for changing or escaping the sun, but the Azimut feels cosier, while the Pershing feels more expansive.

Passenger Capacity and Group Size

With 11 guests, the Azimut 39 is ideal for slightly larger groups. The Pershing 46, at 10 passengers, still accommodates a full party but may feel more exclusive. If you're planning a family outing or a gathering of friends, both boats work well. For larger groups, consider our [Mangusta 80](/blog/azimut-39-vs-pershing-46-marbella/) which holds 12 guests. Always check capacity limits to ensure comfort and safety.

Pricing and Value

The Azimut 39 has transparent, fixed pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €1,149 for 4 hours, €1,649 for 6 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All rates include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. The Pershing 46's price is on request via WhatsApp, typically reflecting its premium sport cruiser status. For budget-conscious groups, the Azimut offers excellent value. For a performance upgrade, the Pershing justifies its cost with speed and style. Explore more [charter options in Marbella](/blog/azimut-39-vs-pershing-46-marbella/) to compare.

Which Boat Should You Choose?

Choose the Azimut 39 if you want a social, affordable day out with up to 11 guests, with a flybridge for views and a relaxed pace. Opt for the Pershing 46 if speed and sporty aesthetics are your priority, and you're happy to request a custom quote. Both boats offer the same inclusions and depart from Puerto Banús, so your experience will be top-tier regardless. For a different vibe, check out our [Azimut 58 Flybridge](/blog/azimut-39-vs-pershing-46-marbella/) for more space.

Frequently asked questions

Which boat is faster, the Azimut 39 or Pershing 46?

The Pershing 46 is generally faster due to its sportier design and larger engines, reaching top speeds around 38-40 knots. The Azimut 39 cruises at about 28-30 knots, making the Pershing the better choice for thrill-seekers.

How many people can the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 accommodate?

The Azimut 39 holds up to 11 passengers, while the Pershing 46 accommodates up to 10. Both boats are certified for their maximum capacities, so you can bring a group of friends or family without worry.

What is included in the price for each boat?

Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Azimut 39 has fixed pricing from €749/2h to €2,299/8h; the Pershing 46 requires a request via WhatsApp.

Which boat is more spacious for a day charter?

The Pershing 46 offers more deck space and a larger cockpit due to its 14m length, making it feel roomier. The Azimut 39 is slightly smaller but still comfortable, with a focus on social seating and a sunpad.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on these charters?

Yes, you can bring additional food and drinks. However, the charter already includes a selection of beverages and light snacks. Check with us via WhatsApp if you have special requests.

Which boat is better for a party or celebration?

The Azimut 39, with capacity for 11 guests and a sociable layout, is ideal for parties. The Pershing 46, though slightly smaller in capacity, offers a sportier vibe and more deck space for mingling.

Do both boats depart from Puerto Banús?

Yes, both the Azimut 39 and Pershing 46 depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. This gives you easy access to the Costa del Sol's best spots like Cabopino, Estepona, and the Marbella coastline.

How do I book the Pershing 46 if its price is on request?

Simply contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date and duration. We'll respond with availability and a tailored quote. The Azimut 39 can be booked directly online with fixed rates.
